/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 107 • Updated: 2026-07-04 17:34 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

The dangers of leveraged trading are real. I think short sellers naturally formed a cabal as their interests were naturally all in line and they do have strength in numbers. They got too cocky and never really had a gameplan to counter the fact that someone could actually show up and want to turn a company around. Short sellers who try to bite off more than they can chew are like snakes who try to swallow prey that is too big and it gets stuck in their throats, unable to swallow. They inadvertently create high-value deals for someone like RC. They find themselves trapped in a turnaround story with no way to exit the trade. And with retail eyes all over the play, they really can't try to negotiate their way out of it. Doug looks so rough here because even the DTCC has been ousted in the fraud. So this is a complete embarrassment for the U.S. capital markets, and we even saw Tricia Rothschild come out from behind the curtain to help conceal the systemic naked short selling scheme.
It takes a lot of time and capital to create billions of dollars. You can't just put a company together overnight. You can, however, short sell a multi billion dollar company into the ground and STEAL all of the equity for yourself through counterfeiting quite easily. That's why they do it. Unfortunately for them, naked shorting is a trading strategy that exposes you to losses that can far exceed the amount of the original investment/exposure.

They were giving money away for pennies because the barcode algo was taking the price to zero and was not phased in any way to price in what was about to happen. If the price was allowed to run back in February 6 2023, who knows where we could have ended up. Someone was getting squeezed back then and they decided to double, triple, quadruple down on the play because they were already bust. BBBYQ was already infiltrated and sabotaged and the short interests were already well outside any semblance of prudence by risk management standards. At least for me, that totally evaporated any and all romantic or family-starting instincts within me, for a long time. My only priority for years was finding stability and securing solid income and investments. After all, I personally watched multiple friends in college flunk out of our degree program after becoming depressed after a bad break up. Why would I risk dating when a bad breakup could end up in me throwing away my GPA, and internship/job prospects, as a potential result?

Now that I have stable income and a solid portfolio, now that I have breathing room, it's uncomfortably apparent how unnecessary the whole ordeal was and how much happier everyone in the situation would be, if boomers weren't convinced en masse that an adult kid asking for money in college was, in every case, a parasitic leech.

ETF outflows hit $4.5b in june but that capital rotated to semis, not cash. the entities selling BTC face quarterly redemptions and compliance committees armed with citi's $82k target. the entities buying have no reporting requirements and no redemption pressure. LTH SOPR at 0.615 last printed in july 2023 at $25k-$31k before the run to $73k. forced sellers are finite. voluntary buyers at record size are not.

62444168
>>62443858
I don't think this is that true anymore, modern AI overlaps self adversarial verification loops to the point where errors are more or less eliminated.

ie. Get 1 AI to code a program, sure there might be bugs, but get another AI to run over night generating test cases, in a loop and testing, supplying feedback to the first AI to make edits etc.

For the price of a human developer for 8 hours of work, you can definitely get more value for money running this setup for 8 hours.


The idea of "I asked chatgpt and it told me there are the wrong number of R's in strawberry" is like 2023 thinking.

Now adays, given enough time the Ai will write a python script that will remove all r's in a word, count length and determine how many Rs went missing. It's capable of cyclically problem solving reasoning and building and running tools to verify its own answers autonomously.


It still makes mistakes but so do people.

item.title: Post-work society — 14 replies — 7.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post-work society

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-04 15:40 UTC

OP

Am I correct in predicting that in the future only a few people will have a traditional job?

There's a guy who makes My Little Pony fleshlights out of silicone and sells them at 500 dollars a pop.
A lot of guys stream on Kick and make more than doctors.
Millions of young women shake their ass on Tiktok and Chaturbate, again making more than doctors.
The list goes on.
I'm starting to see a phenomenon where people no longer go to school, they all find some online niche to fill and then get rich.

Is this what everyone will be doing in 2050? Become an entertainer or die?
I've always hated the right-wing attitude that everyone has to make their own money. I want UBI, not this shit.

Early replies (first 3)

62445033
>the pedo elite won't give us UBI
>instead they give us platforms like Onlyfans where we humiliate ourselves for the right to continue existing

They're making fun of us.

62445042
We don't know how to transition to a world where people access shared wealth. It's a very difficult coordination problem because everyone wants as much wealth as possible. So we're starting to use labor as an artificial need signal. I.e. if you need the wealth really badly, jump through these hoops, and you will prove yourself deserving of that wealth. Even the Soviets forced people to make surplus steel because they didn't know how to just give wealth without using labor as a filter. Unfortunately, this means that in the limit 99% of labor will be pretty much useless.

62445052
>>62445042

But the system isn't meritocratic. I see people far less deserving than me making a thousand times what I make.
I've spent 10 years studying, learning skills, applying for jobs etc. and I'm still in square one. Then there are IQ 60 schizophrenics who make an account on Kick and people watch them because they're entertaining and crazy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445065
>>62445052
So your jealous ,,,,sucks to be you i guess there's always gonna be someone with more than you

62445103
>>62445052
No it is, you simply can't learn the skills that that low iq has, so you are not deserving. You aren't as all round as you make yourself out to be.

62445261
>>62445031
all the shit you listed is the byproduct of idiots having too much spare money
one good market correction will end all of these with a vengeance

62445375
>>62445031
UBI is just a pipedream. The math doesn't work. And the social consequences are devastating. If you give people large handouts they turn to drugs and rot. Really small amounts, like $500 a month or something are a little more mixed, but that's effectively what you have with current welfare programs and also not what you're describing. Outside of experiments you have natural observations of what happens when people receive guaranteed income in different amounts by looking at Indian reservations with casinos of differing sizes. The most successful have tribe members guaranteed six figures a year for life, and the results are still mostly a bunch of layabout alcoholics, though they can afford medical treatments and stuff.

>I'm starting to see a phenomenon where people no longer go to school, they all find some online niche to fill and then get rich

If you look at rich people a lot of them will have done this. But of people not going to school very few will be able to get rich doing this. It will be more common for talented people to ignore college, but this is for a single, sub single digit percentage.

62445416
>>62445033
it's the equivalent of all these scifi dystopian stories where they go do games and contests to get out of the rat race. but might just be worse because not even in those stories are they doing weird degrading sexual humiliation jobs

62445425
>>62445065
gay id spotted

62445434
civilizations across the centuries have had many periods in time in which the masses became too indulgent, hedonistic, with salivating base desires. the issue is that the times in which there is a balance do not last as long as when times are overly strict/repressive and overly indulgent/hedonistic. Both extremes lead to inequality of disproportionate amount in which the humans at their breaking point eventually demand change, swinging the pendulum again. will humans ever be able to achieve balance for longer periods of time? or will the monkey brain always win out no matter what? we need to rid ourselves of the monkey within.

62445439
>>62445031
>A lot of guys stream on Kick and make more than doctors.

Yeah no

62445443
>>62445261
True

62445460
>>62445031
If the dollar loses it's reserve status and we revert back to a Net-Zero trade balance you are just going to be horribly impoverished as the East begins to revalue their own currencies to be able to afford to consume their own production. Most people only have exchange value in the form of dollars, and if those dollars lose this exchange value you'll find it very difficult to continue consuming because things like gasoline and food will be bid up to prices that maybe your "service job" will not be able to pay for. People's ability to actually pay for services will be diminished. Real wealth will come in the form of productive labor and production. So don't go thinking we are post-scarcity and that nothing matters. A Greater Depression is just around the corner, and if you don't have exchange value in the form of dollars and you can't find someone to pay you for your labor then the only other option is selling your cornhole to rich tourists.

62445499
>>62445031
>2050
>UBI
You don't need to wait on either of those. /biz/ was part of the reason I spotted a dip that then did 1000x in my first year of looking at assets. Even $1 becomes over $1 million if doubled again and again, with the task being to find 20 assets that double in price. Keep your eye out for opportunity and you can do far better than being an entertainer or waiting on someone to solve poverty.
